title: PICARD: Parsing Incrementally for Constrained Auto-Regressive Decoding from Language Models
claim: Incremental parsing rejects inadmissible tokens during decoding; turns fine-tuned T5 (up to 3B) from passable into then-SOTA on Spider/CoSQL. (The 12%->2% Spider-dev execution-error figure is from the paper body, verified 2026-07-09 in reports/lit-structured-parsing-and-inner-symbolic.md.)

title: Spider: A Large-Scale Human-Labeled Dataset for Complex and Cross-Domain Semantic Parsing and Text-to-SQL
claim: 10,181 questions / 5,693 SQL on 200 DBs, cross-domain; best 2018 model 12.4% exact match on database split - cross-schema generalization is the hard axis.

title: Can LLM Already Serve as A Database Interface? A BIg Bench for Large-Scale Database Grounded Text-to-SQLs (BIRD)
claim: On realistic large dirty databases ChatGPT execution accuracy 40.08% vs human 92.96% - headline Spider numbers do not transfer to realistic stores.

title: CodeS: Towards Building Open-source Language Models for Text-to-SQL
claim: Open 1B-15B models with SQL-centric incremental pretraining + schema-linking prompts + bidirectional augmentation reach SOTA accuracy AND robustness (Spider, BIRD, Spider-Syn/Realistic/DK, Dr.Spider) at much smaller size than GPT-4-based pipelines.

title: Reliable Answers for Recurring Questions: Boosting Text-to-SQL Accuracy with Template Constrained Decoding
claim: Mining NL->SQL templates from history + fine-tuned template selection + grammar-constrained decoding: headline claim is up to +36 points execution accuracy over ICL and 2.2x lower latency on matched (recurring) queries; matched-BIRD execution accuracy varies ~83.6-89.2% across reported settings/models (corrected 2026-07-11 per external review - do not round to 'at the 0.90 bar'; no S2-style confidence bound is reported).

title: Robust Text-to-SQL Generation with Execution-Guided Decoding
claim: Conditioning decoding on execution of partial programs universally improves 4 architectures on WikiSQL/ATIS/GeoQuery; 83.8% execution accuracy WikiSQL (then-SOTA).

title: Natural Language to Code Translation with Execution (MBR-EXEC)
claim: Selecting among sampled programs by execution-result agreement on test inputs significantly outperforms all non-execution selection methods. LIMIT (clarified 2026-07-11 per external review): agreement over a small set of test inputs approximates semantic equivalence; the vote can select a majority cluster of spurious (consistently-wrong) programs - a selector, not a correctness check.

title: LEVER: Learning to Verify Language-to-Code Generation with Execution
claim: A trained verifier over (NL, program, execution result) improves code-davinci-002 by 4.6-10.9% across 4 language-to-code benchmarks, then-SOTA; verifier sees execution semantics, not just text.

title: TinyGSM: achieving >80% on GSM8k with small language models
claim: 1.3B generator + 1.3B verifier producing Python solutions (executed) reach 81.5% GSM8K, beating the GPT-3.5 teacher (77.4%) and the prior 34B minimum for 80% - small-scale existence proof of NL->program->execute->verify.

title: STaR-SQL: Self-Taught Reasoner for Text-to-SQL
claim: Self-taught reasoning traces for text-to-SQL: 86.6% execution accuracy on Spider, +31.6 over few-shot, +18.0 over direct fine-tune (KB record doi_10.18653_v1_2025.acl-long.1187; NOT re-fetched this pass).

title: Grammar-Constrained Decoding for Structured NLP Tasks without Finetuning
claim: Input-dependent formal grammars constrain off-the-shelf LMs; grammar-constrained LMs substantially outperform unconstrained LMs and can beat task-specific fine-tuned models on IE/ED/constituency parsing; valuable where training data is scarce.

title: Grammar Prompting for Domain-Specific Language Generation with Large Language Models
claim: Predict a minimally-sufficient BNF grammar first, then generate constrained by it; competitive few-shot DSL generation on SMCalFlow/Overnight/GeoQuery/PDDL/SMILES.

title: Mitigating Bias in Locally Constrained Decoding via Tractable Proposals
claim: Studies the sampling bias of locally constrained decoding (myopic token masking) relative to the true grammar-conditioned distribution, over finite-automaton constraint classes, and corrects it with SMC-style tractable proposals converging with far fewer particles (function calling, SQL). SCOPE (clarified 2026-07-11 per external review): a distributional-fidelity result on the constraint classes studied - it does NOT show ordinary local constrained decoding is unsafe/incorrect in general, nor that global decoding is cheap for arbitrary grammars.

title: Constrained Language Models Yield Few-Shot Semantic Parsers
claim: Map NL to a controlled canonical sublanguage of English (grammar-constrained), then deterministically to logical forms; effective few-shot semantic parsing with minimal data.

title: Semantic Parsing via Paraphrasing
claim: Canonical utterances paired with logical forms + a paraphrase model absorb NL variation; the canonical answer to lexical-gap failure, competitive on WebQuestions/Free917.

title: BERT for Joint Intent Classification and Slot Filling
claim: 110M BERT jointly fine-tuned for intent+slot significantly improves intent accuracy, slot F1, and sentence-level semantic frame accuracy on ATIS/Snips. METRIC DISCIPLINE (corrected 2026-07-11 per external review): the ~96-97% figures are intent accuracy / slot F1, NOT whole-frame exactness; sentence-level semantic-frame accuracy (the exactness-shaped metric) is ~88.2% ATIS / ~92.8% Snips (paper body).

title: Measuring Compositional Generalization: A Comprehensive Method on Realistic Data (CFQ)
claim: Maximum-compound-divergence splits: state-of-the-art seq2seq collapses on compositional generalization; strong negative correlation between compound divergence and accuracy.

title: COGS: A Compositional Generalization Challenge Based on Semantic Interpretation
claim: Transformers/LSTMs: 96-99% in-distribution vs 16-35% on compositional generalization split, +-6-8% seed sensitivity - structural (role/argument) generalization is the systematic weakness.

title: Evaluating the Impact of Model Scale for Compositional Generalization in Semantic Parsing
claim: Fine-tuning shows FLAT OR NEGATIVE scaling to 11B on OOD compositional generalization; ICL scales positively but underperforms much smaller fine-tuned models - scale alone does not fix the failure class.

title: Improving Compositional Generalization with Latent Structure and Data Augmentation
claim: Induce a (quasi-synchronous) grammar over training data, recombine to synthesize augmentation, fine-tune T5: SOTA on comp-gen benchmarks incl. natural-language variation - the measured productive use of grammar induction for parsing robustness.

title: Learning Compositional Rules via Neural Program Synthesis
claim: Synthesizing an explicit rule system (grammar) from few examples beats neural meta-learning on SCAN and number-word translation - grammar induction works at tiny scale when the target system is genuinely rule-like.

title: Towards Robustness of Text-to-SQL Models against Synonym Substitution (Spider-Syn)
claim: Removing explicit NL-schema lexical correspondence via synonym substitution dramatically drops learned parser accuracy; synonym annotations on schema recover more than adversarial training.

title: Dr.Spider: A Diagnostic Evaluation Benchmark towards Text-to-SQL Robustness
claim: 17 perturbation sets over DB/NL/SQL: even the most robust model drops 14.0% overall and 50.7% on the hardest perturbation - paraphrase/perturbation stress is the standard modern parser-robustness instrument.

title: A Survey of Text-to-SQL in the Era of LLMs: Where are we, and where are we going?
claim: Lifecycle survey; names NL ambiguity, under-specification, and schema linking (NL expression -> schema element/value grounding) as the persistent core difficulties; systematizes error-diagnosis practice.

title: On the Foundations of Noise-free Selective Classification
claim: Formalizes the risk-coverage trade-off and near-optimal strategies - the theoretical frame for measuring an abstaining parser.

title: Selective Classification for Deep Neural Networks
claim: Set a desired risk level, reject as needed to guarantee it with high confidence (e.g. 2% top-5 ImageNet error at ~60% coverage) - the risk-controlled selective-prediction recipe the S2 gate maps onto.

title: Selective Question Answering under Domain Shift
claim: Softmax confidence degrades under domain shift; a trained calibrator answers 56% of questions at 80% accuracy vs 48% for softmax - calibration must be trained/tested under the shift you will face.

title: Calibrated Interpretation: Confidence Estimation in Semantic Parsing
claim: Calibration of semantic parsers varies across models and datasets; provides confidence-based challenge splits and a calibration-metric library for parsing - parser confidence is usable but not free.

title: Adaptation with Self-Evaluation to Improve Selective Prediction in LLMs (ASPIRE)
claim: Parameter-efficient tuning for task + self-evaluation improves selective prediction (CoQA AUACC 91.23->92.63, AUROC 74.61->80.25) - selective prediction is trainable into small hosts.

title: Conformal Language Modeling
claim: Calibrated stopping + rejection rules over sampled generations give a distribution-free guarantee that the returned set contains an acceptable answer with high probability, with small sets in practice.

title: Mitigating LLM Hallucinations via Conformal Abstention
claim: Conformal calibration of a self-consistency-style match score yields an abstention policy with a rigorous bound on error (hallucination) rate (incl. high-probability risk-control variants), less conservative than log-prob baselines - the guarantee shape the S2 fail-closed gate needs. CENTRAL CAVEAT (elevated 2026-07-11 per external review): valid only under exchangeability and for the chosen loss/match function; the paper notes self-consistency cannot detect a model consistently confident in the same wrong answer - exactly the systematic ROLE_DIR-inversion case.

title: Conformal Prediction for Natural Language Processing: A Survey
claim: Model-agnostic, distribution-free uncertainty sets/guarantees for NLP; catalogues methods and the exchangeability caveats that bound their validity.

title: Zero and Few-shot Semantic Parsing with Ambiguous Inputs (AmP)
claim: Across 5 attested ambiguity types, large pre-trained models are poor at representing the distribution of possible meanings unless ambiguity is deliberately attested in the prompt/data - ambiguity is a property of NL the parser cannot resolve alone.

title: Did You Ask a Good Question? A Cross-Domain Question Intention Classification Benchmark for Text-to-SQL (TriageSQL)
claim: First cross-domain answerable-vs-4-kinds-of-unanswerable benchmark; RoBERTa baseline only 60% F1 - detecting should-abstain inputs is itself hard.

title: Know What I don't Know: Handling Ambiguous and Unanswerable Questions for Text-to-SQL
claim: Parsers produce plausible SQL even for ambiguous/unanswerable questions (6 categories); counterfactual example generation + a detect/localize/explain model beats baselines - the literature's version of the a5-nl confident-wrong class.

title: Boundary-Aware NL2SQL: Integrating Reliability through Hybrid Reward and Data Synthesis (BAR-SQL)
claim: SFT + GRPO with a task-conditioned hybrid reward jointly optimizing execution accuracy AND abstention precision on boundary cases (ambiguity, schema limits); 91.48% avg on Ent-SQL-Bench, claimed above Claude 4.5 Sonnet / GPT-5. Single-group, new benchmark - treat cautiously.

title: Trust but Verify: Prover-Verifier Deliberation for Selective LLM Prediction
claim: Two-model challenge-response deliberation yields a structured confidence signal (~30pp high-confidence-precision gap on GPQA Diamond); signal collapses/inverts when the verifier is weak or out-of-domain - caution for small-model verifier pairs.

title: Synchromesh: Reliable Code Generation from Pre-trained Language Models
claim: Constrained Semantic Decoding enforces syntax/scope/typing/contextual logic during decoding (SQL/Vega-Lite/SMCalFlow). Verified [search] 2026-07-09 in reports/lit-structured-parsing-and-inner-symbolic.md; NOT re-fetched this pass.

title: SynCode: LLM Generation with Grammar Augmentation
claim: General CFG masking removes 96.07% of syntax errors. Verified [search] 2026-07-09 in reports/lit-structured-parsing-and-inner-symbolic.md; NOT re-fetched this pass.

title: XGrammar: Flexible and Efficient Structured Generation Engine
claim: Token-mask caching + pushdown automata make grammar-constrained generation near-zero overhead in serving stacks. Verified [search] 2026-07-09 in reports/lit-structured-parsing-and-inner-symbolic.md; NOT re-fetched this pass.

title: Type-Constrained Code Generation with Language Models
claim: Prefix automata + type inference enforce well-typedness; compilation errors more than halved, repair +37% relative, across model families. Verified [search] 2026-07-09 in reports/lit-structured-parsing-and-inner-symbolic.md; NOT re-fetched this pass.

title: Grammar-Aligned Decoding (ASAp)
claim: Greedy grammar-masking distorts the LM distribution (grammatical but low-quality outputs); ASAp corrects toward the true grammar-conditioned distribution at extra sampling cost. Verified [search] 2026-07-09 in reports/lit-structured-parsing-and-inner-symbolic.md; NOT re-fetched this pass.

title: Autoregressive Entity Retrieval (GENRE)
claim: Constrained beam search over a prefix trie of entity names + deterministic name->ID map - the trie-constrained lexicon-linking shape for gazetteer-miss repair. Carried [memory] from reports/lit-structured-parsing-and-inner-symbolic.md; NOT fetched this pass.
